children.label: A
children.text: when referring to an organizational unit of an institution of higher education, any academic unit that offers one or more academic majors in disciplines or content areas corresponding to the academic subject matter areas in which teachers provide instruction; and

children.label: B
children.text: when referring to a specific academic subject area, the disciplines or content areas in which academic majors are offered by the arts and sciences organizational unit.

children.heading: Children from low-income families
children.label: 2 Children from low-income families
children.text: The term “children from low-income families” means children described in .section 6333(c)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: Core academic subjects
children.label: 3 Core academic subjects
children.text: The term “core academic subjects” means English, reading or language arts, mathematics, science, foreign languages, civics and government, economics, arts, history, and geography.

children.heading: Early childhood educator
children.label: 4 Early childhood educator
children.text: The term “early childhood educator” means an individual with primary responsibility for the education of children in an early childhood education program.

children.heading: Educational service agency
children.label: 5 Educational service agency
children.text: The term “educational service agency” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a high-need local educational agency;

children.label: I
children.text: a high-need school or a consortium of high-need schools served by the high-need local educational agency; or

children.label: II
children.text: as applicable, a high-need early childhood education program;

children.label: iii
children.text: a partner institution;

children.label: iv
children.text: a school, department, or program of education within such partner institution, which may include an existing teacher professional development program with proven outcomes within a four-year institution of higher education that provides intensive and sustained collaboration between faculty and local educational agencies consistent with the requirements of this subchapter; and

children.label: v
children.text: a school or department of arts and sciences within such partner institution; and

children.label: i
children.text: The Governor of the State.

children.label: ii
children.text: The State educational agency.

children.label: iii
children.text: The State board of education.

children.label: iv
children.text: The State agency for higher education.

children.label: v
children.text: A business.

children.label: vi
children.text: A public or private nonprofit educational organization.

children.label: vii
children.text: An educational service agency.

children.label: viii
children.text: A teacher organization.

children.label: ix
children.text: A high-performing local educational agency, or a consortium of such local educational agencies, that can serve as a resource to the partnership.

children.label: x
children.text: A charter school (as defined in ).section 7221i of this title

children.label: xi
children.text: A school or department within the partner institution that focuses on psychology and human development.

children.label: xii
children.text: A school or department within the partner institution with comparable expertise in the disciplines of teaching, learning, and child and adolescent development.

children.label: xiii
children.text: An entity operating a program that provides alternative routes to State certification of teachers.

children.heading: Essential components of reading instruction
children.label: 7 Essential components of reading instruction
children.text: The term “essential components of reading instruction” has the meaning given the term in as such section was in effect on the day before .section 6368 of this titleDecember 10, 2015

children.heading: Exemplary teacher
children.label: 8 Exemplary teacher
children.text: The term “exemplary teacher” has the meaning given the term in as such section was in effect on the day before .section 7801 of this titleDecember 10, 2015

children.heading: High-need early childhood education program
children.label: 9 High-need early childhood education program
children.text: The term “high-need early childhood education program” means an early childhood education program serving children from low-income families that is located within the geographic area served by a high-need local educational agency.

children.label: i
children.text: for which not less than 20 percent of the children served by the agency are children from low-income families;

children.label: ii
children.text: that serves not fewer than 10,000 children from low-income families;

children.label: iii
children.text: that meets the eligibility requirements for funding under the Small, Rural School Achievement Program under ; orsection 7345(b) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: that meets the eligibility requirements for funding under the Rural and Low-Income School Program under ; andsection 7351(b) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: for which there is a high percentage of teachers not teaching in the academic subject areas or grade levels in which the teachers were trained to teach; or

children.label: ii
children.text: for which there is a high teacher turnover rate or a high percentage of teachers with emergency, provisional, or temporary certification or licensure.

children.label: I
children.text: The percentage of students aged 5 through 17 in poverty counted in the most recent census data approved by the Secretary.

children.label: II
children.text: The percentage of students eligible for a free or reduced price school lunch under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1751

children.label: III
children.text: The percentage of students in families receiving assistance under the State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 601

children.label: IV
children.text: The percentage of students eligible to receive medical assistance under the Medicaid program.

children.label: V
children.text: A composite of two or more of the measures described in subclauses (I) through (IV).

children.label: I
children.text: an elementary school, the school serves students not less than 60 percent of whom are eligible for a free or reduced price school lunch under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act; or

children.label: II
children.text: any other school that is not an elementary school, the other school serves students not less than 45 percent of whom are eligible for a free or reduced price school lunch under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act.

children.heading: Designation by the Secretary
children.label: i Designation by the Secretary
children.text: The Secretary may, upon approval of an application submitted by an eligible partnership seeking a grant under this subchapter, designate a school that does not qualify as a high-need school under subparagraph (A) as a high-need school for the purpose of this subchapter. The Secretary shall base the approval of an application for designation of a school under this clause on a consideration of the information required under clause (ii), and may also take into account other information submitted by the eligible partnership.

children.label: aa
children.text: aged 5 through 17 in poverty counted in the most recent census data approved by the Secretary;

children.label: bb
children.text: eligible for a free or reduced price school lunch under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act;

children.label: cc
children.text: in families receiving assistance under the State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act; or

children.label: dd
children.text: eligible to receive medical assistance under the Medicaid program;

children.label: II
children.text: information about the student academic achievement of students at such school; and

children.label: III
children.text: for a secondary school, the graduation rate for such school.

children.label: A
children.text: with specialized education and training in development and education of young children from birth until entry into kindergarten;

children.label: i
children.text: a baccalaureate degree in an academic major in the arts and sciences; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an associate’s degree in a related educational area; and

children.label: C
children.text: who has demonstrated a high level of knowledge and use of content and pedagogy in the relevant areas associated with quality early childhood education.

children.label: A
children.text: High-quality teacher mentoring.

children.label: B
children.text: Periodic, structured time for collaboration with teachers in the same department or field, including mentor teachers, as well as time for information-sharing among teachers, principals, administrators, other appropriate instructional staff, and participating faculty in the partner institution.

children.label: C
children.text: The application of empirically-based practice and scientifically valid research on instructional practices.

children.label: D
children.text: Opportunities for new teachers to draw directly on the expertise of teacher mentors, faculty, and researchers to support the integration of empirically-based practice and scientifically valid research with practice.

children.label: E
children.text: The development of skills in instructional and behavioral interventions derived from empirically-based practice and, where applicable, scientifically valid research.

children.label: i
children.text: model the integration of research and practice in the classroom; and

children.label: ii
children.text: assist new teachers with the effective use and integration of technology in the classroom.

children.label: G
children.text: Interdisciplinary collaboration among exemplary teachers, faculty, researchers, and other staff who prepare new teachers with respect to the learning process and the assessment of learning.

children.label: H
children.text: Assistance with the understanding of data, particularly student achievement data, and the applicability of such data in classroom instruction.

children.label: I
children.text: Regular and structured observation and evaluation of new teachers by multiple evaluators, using valid and reliable measures of teaching skills.

children.heading: Limited English proficient
children.label: 15 Limited English proficient
children.text: The term “limited English proficient” has the meaning given the term “English learner” in .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Parent
children.label: 16 Parent
children.text: The term “parent” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: demonstrating that 80 percent or more of the graduates of the program who intend to enter the field of teaching have passed all of the applicable State qualification assessments for new teachers, which shall include an assessment of each prospective teacher’s subject matter knowledge in the content area in which the teacher intends to teach; or

children.label: I
children.text: using criteria consistent with the requirements for the State report card under before the first publication of such report card; andsection 1022d(b) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: using the State report card on teacher preparation required under , after the first publication of such report card and for every year thereafter; andsection 1022d(b) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: each student in the program to meet high academic standards or demonstrate a record of success, as determined by the institution (including prior to entering and being accepted into a program), and participate in intensive clinical experience;

children.label: ii
children.text: each student in the program preparing to become a teacher who meets the applicable State certification and licensure requirements, including any requirements for certification obtained through alternative routes to certification, or, with regard to special education teachers, the qualifications described in ; andsection 1412(a)(14)(C) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: each student in the program preparing to become an early childhood educator to meet degree requirements, as established by the State, and become highly competent.

children.label: A
children.text: apply rigorous, systematic, and objective methodology to obtain reliable and valid knowledge relevant to education activities and programs;

children.label: B
children.text: present findings and make claims that are appropriate to, and supported by, the methods that have been employed; and

children.label: i
children.text: use of systematic, empirical methods that draw on observation or experiment;

children.label: ii
children.text: use of data analyses that are adequate to support the general findings;

children.label: iii
children.text: reliance on measurements or observational methods that provide reliable and generalizable findings;

children.label: iv
children.text: strong claims of causal relationships, only with research designs that eliminate plausible competing explanations for observed results, such as, but not limited to, random-assignment experiments;

children.label: v
children.text: presentation of studies and methods in sufficient detail and clarity to allow for replication or, at a minimum, to offer the opportunity to build systematically on the findings of the research;

children.label: vi
children.text: acceptance by a peer-reviewed journal or critique by a panel of independent experts through a comparably rigorous, objective, and scientific review; and

children.label: vii
children.text: consistency of findings across multiple studies or sites to support the generality of results and conclusions.

children.heading: Professional development
children.label: 19 Professional development
children.text: The term “professional development” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Scientifically valid research
children.label: 20 Scientifically valid research
children.text: The term “scientifically valid research” includes applied research, basic research, and field-initiated research in which the rationale, design, and interpretation are soundly developed in accordance with principles of scientific research.

children.label: A
children.text: includes clear criteria for the selection of teacher mentors who will provide role model relationships for mentees, which criteria shall be developed by the eligible partnership and based on measures of teacher effectiveness;

children.label: B
children.text: provides high-quality training for such mentors, including instructional strategies for literacy instruction and classroom management (including approaches that improve the schoolwide climate for learning, which may include positive behavioral interventions and supports);

children.label: C
children.text: provides regular and ongoing opportunities for mentors and mentees to observe each other’s teaching methods in classroom settings during the day in a high-need school in the high-need local educational agency in the eligible partnership;

children.label: D
children.text: provides paid release time for mentors, as applicable;

children.label: E
children.text: provides mentoring to each mentee by a colleague who teaches in the same field, grade, or subject as the mentee;

children.label: i
children.text: teaching and learning;

children.label: ii
children.text: assessment of student learning;

children.label: iii
children.text: the development of teaching skills through the use of instructional and behavioral interventions; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the improvement of the mentees’ capacity to measurably advance student learning; and

children.label: i
children.text: common planning time or regularly scheduled collaboration for the mentor and mentee; and

children.label: ii
children.text: joint professional development opportunities.

children.label: A
children.text: for one academic year, teaches alongside a mentor teacher, who is the teacher of record;

children.label: B
children.text: receives concurrent instruction during the year described in subparagraph (A) from the partner institution, which courses may be taught by local educational agency personnel or residency program faculty, in the teaching of the content area in which the teacher will become certified or licensed;

children.label: C
children.text: acquires effective teaching skills; and

children.label: i
children.text: attains full State certification or licensure and, with respect to special education teachers, meets the qualifications described in ; andsection 1412(a)(14)(C) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: acquires a master’s degree not later than 18 months after beginning the program.

children.label: A
children.text: increase student learning, achievement, and the ability to apply knowledge;

children.label: B
children.text: effectively convey and explain academic subject matter;

children.label: C
children.text: effectively teach higher-order analytical, evaluation, problem-solving, and communication skills;

children.label: i
children.text: are based on empirically-based practice and scientifically valid research, where applicable, related to teaching and learning;

children.label: ii
children.text: are specific to academic subject matter; and

children.label: iii
children.text: focus on the identification of students’ specific learning needs, particularly students with disabilities, students who are limited English proficient, students who are gifted and talented, and students with low literacy levels, and the tailoring of academic instruction to such needs;

children.label: E
children.text: conduct an ongoing assessment of student learning, which may include the use of formative assessments, performance-based assessments, project-based assessments, or portfolio assessments, that measures higher-order thinking skills (including application, analysis, synthesis, and evaluation);

children.label: F
children.text: effectively manage a classroom, including the ability to implement positive behavioral interventions and support strategies;

children.label: G
children.text: communicate and work with parents, and involve parents in their children’s education; and

children.label: H
children.text: use, in the case of an early childhood educator, age-appropriate and developmentally appropriate strategies and practices for children in early childhood education programs.
